TITLE: Aditya Roy Kapur inaugurates SRM University's Decade Edition of ...

CONTENT: Chennai 's biggest college fest is back again , this time with an even bigger bang than before . Milan ' 17 , SRM University 's flagship fest , is a National level Sports and Cultural fest which hosts colleges from around the country . New Delhi India , Mar 9 ( ANI-NewsVoir ) : Chennai 's biggest college fest is back again , this time with an even bigger bang than before . Milan ' 17 , SRM University 's flagship fest , is a National level Sports and Cultural fest which hosts colleges from around the country . This year , the fest will kick off with Aditya Roy Kapur lighting the lamp . This actor has stunned fans with his movies before and his latest release " OK Jaanu " won many hearts across the country . This being said , he 's just the first in major celebrity in the line-up . The inauguration ceremony is followed by Musical Night which hosts performances from Palak and Palash Muchhal as well as Anushka Manchanda . Palak and Palash , the sibling duo , have made a name for themselves with hits from @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ " M.S. Dhoni : The Untold Story " . They are also known for their philanthropic work , performing stage shows across India and abroad to raise funds for poor children in need of financial assistance . The second act of the evening , Anushka Manchanda , has been in the industry for over a decade and does n't seem to be slowing down . This Delhi girl has been nominated for countless awards for her songs in Hindi as well as Tamil . Chennai 's weather will be getting just a little bit hotter once she steps on stage . The musical night starts 7 p.m. onwards . Day 2 brings the much awaited " Vogue Way " which will leave the ramp on fire and the audience thirsty for more . These young models and designers are not to be taken lightly as they really know what they 're doing . They aspire to walk amongst the best and their attitude makes it all evident . The third day holds the biggest concert in the city , when Vishal Dadlani and Shekhar Ravjiani perform live @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ they just let their music do the talking . These multilingual artistes have taken the industry by storm with their back to back hits . Their most recent chart topping hits include songs from " Befikre " and " Sultan " . The concert starts at 8 p.m. on the 10th of March . Day 4 brings Choreonite , which is a dance competition . Though the participants will be the ones on stage performing , it 'll be really hard to not groove amidst all the thumping beats and tapping feet . It also brings Broadway , which showcases upcoming actors . This drama competition showcases various acts prepared by students that are sure to leave the audience entranced . And finally , the last day brings us East India Comedy , a comedy group that has transformed the way we view stand-up comedy . Sahil Shah and Saurabh Pant give their take on the issues that we deal with in our day to day life and it 's bound to leave your stomachs turning with laughter . Milan is a fest filled with fun , @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ and sports events beautifully in an event that is organized the students . ( ANI-NewsVoir )

URL: http://www.edmontonsun.com/2014/07/10/redblacks-head-coach-rick-campbell-starting-qb-henry-burris-relish-challenge-of-building-new-franchise
COUNTRY: ca
CONTINENT: America

TITLE: RedBlacks head coach Rick Campbell, starting QB Henry Burris ...

CONTENT: Topics To be there in the beginning , as a Day One member of the Ottawa Rouge et Noir , is to be involved in something special . People involved are starting to see that now . But Rick Campbell saw that going in . It 's why he took the job . Henry Burris maybe did n't see it in the beginning . At first he saw it as another job for , as he put it to your correspondent Thursday , " Mr. Clean . " But he sees it now and is living it for all it 's worth . Ottawa still has n't won a football game but the Redblacks are becoming a special Canadian sports story . Wednesday in Ottawa the team held an evening in their new stadium , with more than 10,000 season-ticket holders in attendance to look the place over , meet the players , get autographs , check out their seats and generally just feel the warm and fuzzies of being back in the CFL . But it was the players who flew to Edmonton Thursday morning on the high of living an extraordinary experience and opening at @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ 're living a moment . And it really is why the former Harry Ainlay High School player and son of the legendary Eskimos coach and GM Hugh Campbell , took the job in the first place . " There really is a whole adventure to this , " he said . " When I got hired , there were six total players and no coaches . " My comment when I got there was , ' Hey , at least we 're not going to take a penalty for too many men on the field . ' " Marcel was sitting there by himself last year at this time , " he said of GM Marcel Desjardins . " There 's not many times in sports where you can just walk in and not fight old culture in the way things are done . In this situation , it 's what we make it . " That 's what the whole attraction right now is , for the coaches and the players . It is what we make it . We 're going to @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ from scratch is something few get to experience . " That was the attraction to me . You get a chance to establish all the people you hire and the culture and everything that happens with it . " You 'd expect that there would be hiccups . Like somebody forgetting to order the footballs . Or showing up at the first game and needing to borrow tape from the home team . But nothing like that has happened . " When I got there we had a cage with our equipment of which there was one box . We did n't even have footballs . We just had a couple of shirts . It 's gone from that at Christmastime , literally , to where we are . " There really has n't been anything where we said ' Boy , we really screwed this up ! " " That 's boring , " suggested your correspondent . " Hey , there was one box when I got there ! " said Campbell . " It 's been well @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ fly-by-night thing . They 've invested seven years trying to get this together . There 's a lot invested financially and emotionally . They 're all local guys to Ottawa . We 're taking our lead from these guys . They 're there for the long haul and we want to be there for the long haul . " We 've grown fast . We knew we 'd have to catch up fast to the other teams . And that 's what we 're on a mission to do is get as good as we can as fast as we can . " It started with GM Desjardins doing an exceptional job laying down the framework . Of course , all he had to do to beat a previous Ottawa GM was not draft a dead guy . " I knew Marcel , but I did n't know him really well . When I went out and interviewed , I just threw everything out that I had to say . Philosophically we seemed to align in what we believed and what we wanted to do and @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ " We 're just trying to win games right now . The first time we ever met as a group in mini-camp , we talked about not being an expansion team . We have too many veteran coaches and too many veteran players . We wanted to throw our hat in the ring just like everybody else in the league and try win as many games as we can . And our players have had that attitude so far . " We feel we have some pieces in place here . So ' Why not us ? ' in terms of going out and winning games . " The most obvious piece is Smilin ' Hank . " You know what ? It 's been fun . It 's really been fun , " said the 17-year veteran who took the Hamilton Tiger-Cats to the Grey Cup last year , only to be released Jan. 30 and quickly signed to a three-year deal by Ottawa four days later . " I think for my whole , entire career I 've been like Mr. Clean . I @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ in organizations . " Going to Saskatchewan in 2000 to get that organization turned around . Then coming to Calgary after the dog and pony show that was down there and helping turn things around there . Then going to Hamilton and helping turn that around . " I love the challenges . My favourite movie is Dodgeball for a reason . It 's the perfect underdog story . To be part of something like that , there 's nothing like it . Anybody can walk into something that 's successful and maintain that . But if you bring something from the bottom to the top and see all the hard work you put in be all worth it in the end ... " That 's why , in the celebrations in Calgary in ' 08 , guys were crying , because we went through so much just to get there . " Just imagine to do it in this situation . This is different . I mean , this is a different challenge . This is from scratch . " To have a chance @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ , as far as far as bringing a first-year team , a first-year organization and have a chance to win a championship ... " Man , that would be a truly special thing to achieve , especially in a place where they have n't had football since ' 05. " said the quarterback who drove Ottawa to three touchdowns on his first three possessions in their opener in Winnipeg , only to end up losing 36-28 . " Just seeing the reception we had Wednesday night , having an open house with over 10,000 fans crowding in and we 're signing autographs just showed the excitement of being there in Ottawa . " My gosh , that was electric back there . Just to see so many people come out and pour out their excitement and their readiness for that first home game . It just showed us the excitement of bringing football back to Ottawa . " It 's like Winnipeg , when they got the Jets back in hockey . People are truly excited . It 's like I 'm telling everybody : ' @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ game come the 18th against the Argos , you 're going to be missing something . ' Just the electricity and the atmosphere that 's going to be in that city . We got just a taste of it at that event before we came here . " Burris believes the excitement is going to translate to success on the field . " The players are feeding off of it . Every time we come together as a team we talk about getting a win for the organization . But even above that , we talk about getting a win for this city that 's had to wait for a long time . " It does n't matter where you go , all around town . From getting a burger somewhere to going out and buying toilet paper . People are talking about , ' Hey , you play for that team . I got my red and black stuff . I got my this and that . ' It just shows you how excited people are in and around Ottawa that football is back . @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ honoured to be there and be a part of this , " said the 10-year veteran of the Calgary Stampeders who also had stints with the Saskatchewan Roughriders and Tiger-Cats , and spent time with the Chicago Bears and Green Bay Packers in the NFL in there , as well . " I saw the guys they picked up in the expansion draft . They brought in some incredible guys , guys who were winners . A lot of good players . I mean , we 've got a good bunch of players . " It 's a first-class organization . The ownership has gone above and beyond to make things so much more accommodating . " We actually feel we 're building a family . That 's what creates successful organizations and teams on the field . You 're not just on the team , you 're part of a family . That 's what the organization is creating back there . " To be there in the beginning , as a Day One member of the Ottawa Rouge et Noir , is to be @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ to see that now . But Rick Campbell saw that going in . It 's why he took the job . Henry Burris maybe did n't see it in the beginning . At first he saw it as another job for , as he put it to your correspondent Thursday , " Mr. Clean . " But he sees it now and is living it for all it 's worth . Ottawa still has n't won a football game but the Redblacks are becoming a special Canadian sports story . Wednesday in Ottawa the team held an evening in their new stadium , with more than 10,000 season-ticket holders in attendance to look the place over , meet the players , get autographs , check out their seats and generally just feel the warm and fuzzies of being back in the CFL . But it was the players who flew to Edmonton Thursday morning on the high of living an extraordinary experi

URL: https://www.theweek.co.uk/98416/china-bans-sale-of-older-iphones
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: China 'bans' sale of older iPhones

CONTENT: The ban does not cover the new iPhone XS , iPhone XS Plus or iPhone XR , as they were " not yet available when Qualcomm filed its lawsuit " , CNN says . However , the ban covers between 10% and 15% of all iPhone models currently available for sale in China . The BBC says that " most legal observers had expected China to reject Qualcomm 's request for an injunction " , adding that while the court proceedings had nothing to do with the arrest of Huawei executive Meng Wanzhou , the court 's ruling was something of a " muscle-flexing exercise " for China . Despite the court ruling , Apple will continue to sell " all iPhone models " in China , according to the New York Times . " Qualcomm 's effort to ban our products is another desperate move by a company whose illegal practices are under investigation by regulators around the world , " Apple spokesman Josh Rosenstock said .

URL: https://thestar.com/business/real_estate/2022/05/31/ontarios-politicians-all-tout-the-same-fix-for-the-housing-crisis-supply-supply-supply-but-experts-say-its-not-that-simple.html
COUNTRY: ca
CONTINENT: America

TITLE: Real EstateOntario's politicians all tout the same fix for the housing crisis: supply, supply, supply. But experts say it's not that simple

CONTENT: This copy is for your personal non-commercial use only . To order presentation-ready copies of Toronto Star content for distribution to colleagues , clients or customers , or inquire about permissions/licensing , please go to : **27;488;TOOLONG Ontario 's politicians all tout the same fix for the housing crisis : supply , supply , supply . But experts say it 's not that simple It 's an idea we 've heard throughout this election campaign -- if you build more homes , housing will become more affordable in the GTA . But there are dissenting opinions on how many homes we need and questions about whether flooding the market with more supply would actually bring the price of renting or owning within reach of more people . It 's an attractive idea but it 's an illusion , says Luisa Sotomayor , associate professor at York University 's Faculty of Environmental and Urban Change . " It 's always politically effective to oversimplify a message and this is what 's happening here , " Sotomayor said . " It is never as simple as supply and demand . " Based on the @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Ontario government is likely to begin tackling the housing crisis starting from the most basic economic concept of supply and demand . But experts warn the housing affordability challenge demands a more nuanced understanding of the problem and solutions . " The amount of housing that needs to be built to have an impact on the affordability of the market is too high , " she said She cites a 2019 British study by the UK Collaborative Centre for Housing Evidence that reported computer modelling showed 1 per cent increase in the total housing stock would lower home prices between only 1.5 and 2 per cent . The same study said that , despite a housing surplus in Britain , home prices in some areas -- particularly London -- continued to grow largely because of international investment in the city . But Sotomayor says she does n't expect an influx of supply in Toronto or that any additional homes would be more affordable because boosting construction only serves the interest of one group -- first-time buyers who are n't yet in the market . " @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ this is their retirement and they 're interested in keeping prices high . Also , the real estate industry benefits ; the construction industry ; the suppliers ; the developers ... If prices are actually being impacted , the natural response by the industry would be to slow down the rate of construction , " said Sotomayor . Joel Roberts , a PhD candidate whose dissertation at York 's department of social and political thought , focuses on the drivers of rising Canadian home prices . He says it 's a " false assumption " that simply increasing supply will tamp down prices . You only have to look at the market declines of the last two months . " If prices were being driven by a lack of supply and not the capacity to take on debt or speculation , then interest rates would n't be having this effect , " he said . In the current context of falling prices , Roberts says developers are unlikely to build more housing so policies aimed at boosting supply are unlikely to be effective . But if you @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ need a more targeted approach to increase the number of affordable units . " You can build all the expensive condos or suburban detached homes you like , it wo n't make housing more affordable . What we need is a targeted approach that increases the supply of affordable housing options in particular -- that means supporting non-profit organizations , community land trusts , social housing , or regulating the private market through tools like inclusionary zoning . " He argues homes are n't like other consumer goods because they are an investment or asset . " Asset prices are subject to different dynamics , " says Roberts . Supply and demand models are n't as helpful for understanding asset prices . When thinking about the price of an Apple stock , we usually think about the returns that stock is expected to generate . The same can be said of house prices . Additionally , home prices are tied to big economic factors such as interest rates and future expectations . Voters are going into Thursday 's election with politicians telling them the province @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ . That 's the number recommended on Feb. 8 by the Ontario Progressive Conservative government 's Housing Affordability Task Force , chaired by Jake Lawrence , CEO of Scotiabank Global Banking and Markets . In January , Scotiabank reported that Ontario needs 650,000 more homes to address its share of a 1.8-million Canadian shortfall . That would bring our supply in line with other G7 nations . But Bank of Montreal chief economist Doug Porter noted that , while Canada 's per capitahousing supply is similar to the U.S. , our prices are about 60 per cent higher . Independent economist Will Dunning is firmly in the camp of those who believe a supply shortage has played a significant role in driving up home prices . He says , " Lower interest rates have made it possible for people to pay higher prices but it 's the lack of housing that has caused prices to rise . " In a May report on housing supply , Dunning determined that 19 of 36 Census Metropolitan Areas ( CMAs ) have housing surpluses and 17 have deficits @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ housing and all the surpluses are in medium-density townhouses and high-density apartments . He calculates the Toronto CMA is short 153,942 low-density homes . But a surplus of high- and mid-density housing reduces the overall shortfall to 89,189 units . Dunning says Canada needs to build a total of 3.9 million homes by 2036 -- an average of 258,000 homes per year , compared to the 225,000 it builds now . The annual amount of housing needed will decline toward the end of that period as population growth slows and Canada ages . But until 2026 , we need to be building 275,000 homes a year . The government has estimated Ontario built fewer than 100,000 homes last year . He says that resale home listings and transactions are commonly used to measure housing supply and demand . But those do n't measure the total amount of existing housing or the real demand that should be determined by household formation . Economic conditions such as high employment and wages should be causing more household formation but , he says , a lack of supply is deterring young @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ " Government policies have ignored demand and supply , and focused on the sales transactions , attempting to suppress home-buying , " says Dunning in the report , highlighting the mortgage stress test , which he says uses an unreasonably high interest rate and ignores the income growth that will happen during the initial term of the mortgage . Those policies make the supply problem worse , he says , by reducing pre-construction house and condo sales . But demand finds a way , he says , and the result is housing " whack-a-mole " : Fewer people can buy so there 's more pressure on the rental sector . That , in turn , encourages investor rental purchases and reduces the number of owner-occupied homes . Or , buyers migrate to alternative lenders and end up paying higher interest rates , defeating the point of the policies aimed at deterring demand . Addressing the problem will require tackling a list of existing deterrents to Canada 's housing supply , including delayed building approvals and infrastructure ; government fees on new construction ; mortgage rules and @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ that are most problematic are the ones that do n't really get talked about -- like the way infrastructure is getting financed by adding $160,000 to the cost of a home to finance the installation of roads and sewers . Market prices need to rise to enable the builders to pay those charges . " Anyone can read Conversations , but to contribute , you should be registered Torstar account holder . URL: https://www.philstar.com/business/2018/03/29/1801208/maybank-sees-faster-philippine-gdp-growth
COUNTRY: ph
CONTINENT: Asia

TITLE: Maybank sees faster Philippine GDP growth

CONTENT: On the sidelines of the Invest ASEAN 2018 conference , Maybank International Bank Berhad regional head of economic research Suhaimi Ilias said the gross domestic product ( GDP ) of the Philippines would expand seven percent this year after easing to 6.7 percent in 2017 . SINGAPORE -- The Philippines is poised to be at the forefront in terms of economic growth among members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations ( ASEAN ) with the massive infrastructure buildup being undertaken by the Duterte administration , Maybank Kim Eng Group said . On the sidelines of the Invest ASEAN 2018 conference , Maybank International Bank Berhad regional head of economic research Suhaimi Ilias said the gross domestic product ( GDP ) of the Philippines would expand seven percent this year after easing to 6.7 percent in 2017 . The Philippines has booked 76 quarters of uninterrupted growth as GDP grew 6.6 percent in the fourth quarter of last year from seven percent in the third quarter . Last year 's GDP growth of 6.7 percent was well within the government 's 6.5 to 7.5 percent target , but was lower than the 6.9 percent @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ . For 2018 , Philippine economic managers see GDP expanding between seven and eight percent . " I think the key factor behind that is we believe we are going to see a much more visible impact of President Duterte 's Build Build Build infrastructure program this year , " he said . The current administration has committed to ramp up infrastructure projects where it intends to spend P8.4 trillion until President Duterte ends his term in 2022 . " The program has been talked about for quite some time but I guess nothing has significantly materialized over the past year . But having said that , the Philippine economy has been doing well The Philippines together with Malaysia , Thailand , Singapore Indonesia , and Vietnam comprise ASEAN-6 . Last year , Vietnam 's GDP growth outpaced that of the Philippines at 6.8 percent . The Philippines is likely to retake the crown this year as the projected seven-percent growth for the country is above the projected 5.2 percent expansion for ASEAN . Ilias said the Philippine economy would continue to @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ , and Japan . He said another positive factor is the implementation of the tax reform law under Republic Act 10963 or the Tax Reform for Acceleration and Inclusion ( TRAIN ) Law that is expected to yield P90 billion in additional revenues . Ilias also doused concerns of overheating as the country 's credit to GDP as well as money supply to GDP ratio remain manageable compared to the levels experienced by ASEAN members during the Asian financial crisis in 1997 and 1998 . URL: http://www.freep.com/story/sports/college/2016/12/03/seidel-wmus-pj-fleck-king-kalamazoo/94894980/
COUNTRY: us
CONTINENT: America

TITLE: Seidel: WMU's PJ Fleck is the King of Kalamazoo

CONTENT: Only one thing ... certain : you have never seen anything quite like this young , energetic coach from Western Michigan . He stood in the middle of Ford Field late Friday night , lifting the Mid-American Conference trophy into the air after the Broncos ' 29-23 victory over Ohio . He held the trophy with two hands , pumping it up and down over his head as if he were doing reps in the weight room . Somehow , this impossible dream became reality . The Broncos are MAC champions for the first time since 1988 . Fleck started hugging people -- players and administrators and boosters and more players -- anybody within reach . The rest is a blur : How he picked up the band director and hoisted him around like a rag doll , and how he ran to the wall at the base of the stands and tried to scale it while giving an impromptu selfie with a fan , and how he stopped and posed for pictures with the cheerleaders , and how he ran to the end zone and started to sing with his players , arm in arm : " Western @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ we bring to you faith untold . " Western Michigan linebacker Robert Spillane celebrates with teammates after his interception in the final seconds of WMU 's 29-23 win over Ohio in the MAC title game Friday at Ford Field . Kirthmon F. Dozier DFP Western Michigan linebacker Robert Spillane celebrates with Eric Assoua after his interception in the final seconds of WMU 's 29-23 win over Ohio in the MAC title game Friday at Ford Field . Kirthmon F. Dozier DFP Ohio safety Kylan Nelson after intercepting a pass by Western Michigan quarterback Zach Terrell during the second half of WMU 's 29-23 win over Ohio in the MAC title game Friday at Ford Field . Kirthmon F. Dozier DFP Ohio safety Kylan Nelson after intercepting a pass by Western Michigan quarterback Zach Terrell during the second half of WMU 's 29-23 win over Ohio in the MAC title game Friday at Ford Field . Kirthmon F. Dozier DFP Ohio safety Kylan Nelson after intercepting a pass by Western Michigan quarterback Zach Terrell during the second half of WMU 's 29-23 win over Ohio in @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ F. Dozier DFP Corey Davis , the star wide receiver , climbed a ladder in front of the stands . Davis was in Fleck 's first recruiting class , which was thrown together in a matter of months after he was hired . Davis was on the team that struggled through a painful 1-11 season -- the foundation , they all call it , the struggle that brought the success . Davis was one of the guys who bought in and stayed when he could have gone to the NFL . As they sang the school 's fight song , Fleck was smiling and clapping and pumping his fist , like he was at a rock concert . Even when he came to a stop , he did n't stop . He tipped his cap and gave his wife a kiss and a hug . Fans leaned over a railing and started to chant : " P.J. ! P.J. ! P.J. ! P.J. ! " Eventually , Fleck reached the locker room , a scene that was captured by an ESPN video camera . He stopped @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ few steps and dived onto his players . They lifted him up like he was in a mosh pit , and he turned upside down and grabbed an overhead pipe and curled himself into the air . He 's climbing all right . There 's no telling where he 's going to end up . Fleck is the hot young coach who brought a championship to Kalamazoo , a culmination of four years of work . He changed the culture , brought in talent , lifted expectations and gave this program a complete face-lift . He convinced this team to believe , and he convinced this school to believe . He convinced this community to believe , dreaming of a championship , and then , they went out and did it . " I feel very good about Coach Fleck , " Beauregard said . " He loves being a Bronco . He loves Western Michigan and our community , and it 's going to take something that comes along that is extra , extra special to be able to not want to continue to be @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ , Fleck made an impassioned plea for the Broncos to go to the Cotton Bowl , and that seems all but certain after Temple beat Navy on Saturday . Eventually , after he seemed to thank every person in Kalamazoo , he ended the news conference and disappeared out the door . He is in a tremendous position . Certainly it would be understandable for him to jump to a major program like Oregon . But if that does n't happen , he can stay at WMU and wait for the perfect offer . He does n't have to jump to a bad Big Ten program , because he 's got it pretty good right now . Great , actually . Still , this guy is a lot to take in , especially from the outside . Maybe all the slogans bother you . Maybe he is too much of a salesman for you . Maybe he 's too hokey for you .

URL: https://www.nigeriatoday.ng/2017/03/rhesus-factor-we-all-goofed/
COUNTRY: ng
CONTINENT: Africa

TITLE: Rhesus factor : We all goofed

CONTENT: Rhesus factor : We all goofed If as a surgeon you have had a working relationship with a colleague for more than twenty years , it is not uncommon to take certain things for granted when he invites you to come and help out . Things like leaving the patient on the operating table and asking the assistant to write up the operation notes while you take a walk becomes commonplace . On certain occasion , the patent is already on the table and on arrival you go straight for the procedure without even saying hello to the medical director(MD) of the hospital . This day was no different . He had been informed of an elective caesarian section the previous day . When he asked of the indication , the reply of the MD was that her first confinement was through a caesarian section . So , there was no big deal . On arrival , the surgeon went in for what he thought would be a simple caesarian section . The shocker came when the baby was brought out ; there was no life in him . The first impulse was to go for @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ and it was n't there ! To make the matter worse , the skin of the dead baby was already peeling off . The umbilical cord was rotten and the liquor was heavily stained with meconium . In clinical terms , it was macerated still birth , meaning that the baby had been dead for more than 48 hours and no one raised an alarm . Well , operating to remove a dead baby is not a misnomer in practice and actually be a life saver in most instances . But a pre operative diagnosis helps everyone to know what they are getting into . Now to the crux of the matter ; the patient had Rhesus disease(RhD) . This lesion goes by various names like rhesus incompatibility , rhesus D haemolytic disease of the newborn , rhesus isoimmunization , erythroblastosis foetalis and so on . This disease results in the destruction of the red blood cells of the baby while still in the womb . Basically , it is an immunological disease that manifests when a mother 's blood is incompatible with that of the baby with respect @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ , there are those who have RhD gene and have the RhD antigen expressed in their red blood cells . If you are one of those ; then you are said to be RHESUS POSITIVE . But if you do not have this antigen in your red cells , you are said to be RHESUS NEGATIVE . The rhesus phenomenon comes into play when a rhesus negative mother conceives a baby who is rhesus positive . During the process of delivery or termination of the pregnancy , the maternal and foetal blood does mix . The foetal blood on entering the maternal circulation would be treated as a foreign body due to the presence of RhD antigen . The maternal immune system would in turn produce antibodies designed to attack and destroy the foetal red cells . Note that this only becomes evident in subsequent pregnancies irrespective of how the index pregnancy ended ; be it normal delivery at term , ectopic pregnancy or even an abortion . Thus during subsequent pregnancies of RhD positive foetus , antibodies from the mother with low molecular weight especially the IgG @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ the red blood cells of foetus leading to their destruction . This condition can be mild , moderately severe or severe . During the process of red cell destruction , the foetus tries to compensate by increasing the rate of red cell production leading to increased presence of immature red cells known as ERYTHROBLASTS in the foetal circulation . This condition is known as erythroblastosis foetalis . In extremely severe case , Rhesus disease can lead to foetal intra uterine death with accumulation of fluid in the babies and when delivered are noted to be bloated ; a condition known as HYDROPS FOETALIS . In our environment , best way to cope with this condition ; which has paid off to a large extent , is not by doing tests to confirm the presence of anti D antibodies in the maternal or foetal blood , but to assume that once a woman is Rhesus negative she is already sensitised . Thus , it is routine for all Rhesus negative mother to be given anti-RhD antibodies to mop up any Rhesus antigen that may have crossed the placenta barrier into @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ at twenty eight weeks of gestation . It is almost mandatory that expectant Rh negative mothers arrange for RHOGAM brand of IgG months before confinement that would be administered as soon as the baby is delivered . This aggressive approach has been of tremendous help in the management of Rhesus phenomenon . Having said this , much there are tests to detect antibodies and their level in the blood of the patients . The most commonly performed test is INDIRECT COOMBS test in which blood is taken from the mother to test for the presence of IgG antibodies that may crossed the placenta into maternal circulation . This is usually a prenatal and pre blood transfusion- for the baby- test . The DIRECT COOMBS TEST is for the baby when an auto immune haemolytic anaemia is present . In our environment it is routine that in all cases of jaundice in the newborn that requires exchange blood transfusion , the mother 's blood will be tested irrespective what the blood group or Rh status is . There are other blood tests that are of importance in the management @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ , a toxic product from red blood cell breakdown that causes yellowish discoloration of the whitish part of the eye and skin , white cell count , platelet , hemoglobin and so on . These enable the caregivers to determine preferred management options to be considered . Ironically in our environment the commonest neonatal scourge is sepsis . Even when the baby is obviously jaundiced , infection is still considered top on the scale of differentials before considering challenges like prematurity and small for gestational age In a resource constraint environment especially with challenged laboratory back up , in severe jaundice and neonatal anaemia , phototherapy and exchange blood transfusion is the gold standard . Antibiotics are given to the babies routinely and n some centres the babies are treated for ' transfusion Malaria ' . These babies are kept under close watch until the level of the serum bilirubin is down to a safe value . This specialized health care is better left in the hands of a paeditrician . Suffice it to say that in established Rhesus negative mother , eternal vigilance through antenatal period to @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ it was unthinkable that in our index case no one who had anything to do with the baby raised an alarm . What an embarrassing goof.

TITLE: Handful $1.69 million Financing. Jennifer Ferguson Filed Jan 5 ...

CONTENT: Handful $1.69 million Financing . Jennifer Ferguson Filed Jan 5 Form D January 5 , 2018 - By Kurt Siggers Handful Financing Handful , Inc. , Corporation just filed form D regarding $1.69 million equity financing . This is a new filing . Handful was able to sell $1.39 million . That is 82.07 % of the fundraising . The total financing amount was $1.69 million . The form was filed on 2018-01-05 . The reason for the financing was : unspecified . The fundraising still has about $302,524 more and is not closed yet . We have to wait more to see if the offering will be fully taken . Handful is based in Oregon . The filler 's business is not disclosed . The form D was signed by Jennifer Ferguson President . The company was incorporated more than five years ago . The filler 's address is : 16200 Sw Pacific Highway , Suite H #115 , Tigard , Or , Oregon , 97224 . Jennifer Ferguson is the related person in the form and it has address : 16200 Sw Pacific Highway , Suite H #115 , Tigard , Or @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ 000172688318000002 . Analysis of Handful Offering On average , companies in the not disclosed sector , sell 67.77 % of the total offering size . Handful sold 82.07 % of the offering . The financing is still open . Could this mean that the trust in Handful is high ? The average investment size for companies in all industries in our database is $3.05 million . The offering was 54.58 % smaller than the average of $3.05 million . Of course this should not be seen as negative . Startups raise funds for a variety of needs and reasons . The minimum investment for this offering was set at $0 . If you know more about the reasons for the financing , please comment below . What is Form D ? What It Is Used For Form D disclosures could be used to track and understand better your competitors . The information in Form D is usually highly confidential for ventures and startups and they do n't like revealing it . This is because it reveals amount raised or planned to be raised as well as @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Entrepreneurs usually want to keep their financing a ' secret ' so they can stay in stealth mode for longer . Why Fundraising Reporting Is Good For Handful Also The Form D signed by Jennifer Ferguson might help Handful , Inc. ' s sector . First , it helps potential customers feel more safe to deal with a firm that is well financed . The odds are higher that it will stay in the business . Second , this could attract other investors such as venture-capital firms , funds and angels . Third , positive PR effects could even bring leasing firms and venture lenders .

URL: http://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/tvandradio/10194813/The-end-of-the-affair-Burton-and-Taylor-to-be-BBC-Fours-last-original-drama.html
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: The end of the affair - Burton and Taylor to be BBC Four's last ...

CONTENT: Burton and Taylor is to be BBC Four 's last original drama . Its series of great British biopics has been cut off in its prime , says Ben Lawrence . There is a scene in BBC Four 's Burton and Taylor when , as she prepares for what would turn out to be a critically mauled run of Private Lives on Broadway , Elizabeth Taylor ( played by Helena Bonham Carter ) stands alone in a large , draughty rehearsal room . She faces a featureless wall and takes a few long drags on a cigarette in a glamorous , introspective way . It 's a lovely moment , capturing Taylor 's desolation at that time , in 1983 . She had hoped that Private Lives would offer the chance for reconciliation with her co-star and former husband Richard Burton ( Dominic West ) . But now she knows she has lost him to his sensible new partner , Sally ; that the days of wine and roses are behind her . That scene is also a perfect example of what the BBC Four biopic has done so well -- the ability to examine the life of a big @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ of the small story , to find the emotional truth behind the public image . Over the past seven years we have learnt that Enid Blyton was a cold , distant mother , that Hughie Green was a sexual predator , that Frankie Howerd lived every day of his life in a state of nervous anxiety . It was Kenneth Williams : Fantabulosa ! ( 2006 ) , a devastatingly bleak look at the comic 's life starring Michael Sheen , that set the template for the channel 's drama output . It showed what could be done with a small budget , minimal sets and a quick turnaround . But Andy De Emmony 's film also set the tone in terms of format : self-contained dramas focusing on very distinct periods in the lives of 20th-century British celebrities -- among them Fanny Cradock , Tony Hancock , Barbara Cartland and Kenny Everett . These were not subjects thought fit for the conventional cinema biopic ; they were not kings and queens , great artists or musicians . In fact , they were often rather suburban tales @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ a bavarois cream pudding in darkest Essex ; a depressed Tony Hancock viciously attacked the bland praise given by his new lover 's provincial father . These dramas showed the drabness of British life in the Fifties , Sixties and Seventies . They were set in the past , but it was a not-too-distant past that audiences could hazily recollect . These biopics often revealed shocking truths about the secret lives of their subjects . In Hattie , the cuddly Carry On star Hattie Jacques carried on with a younger man while her husband , Dad 's Army actor John Le Mesurier , retired courteously to the attic bedroom . In The Curse of Steptoe , one of the finest films in the series , tensions between Steptoe and Son 's Harry H Corbett and Wilfrid Brambell proved almost unbearable viewing . Corbett was a classically trained actor who wanted to be a part of the brave new world of Sixties British theatre , not mugging for laughs in a cramped studio in Shepherds Bush . Brambell was a closeted homosexual whose sudden fame meant he could no longer @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Four biopic gained recognition , so speculation grew . Matt Lucas was reported to be playing Benny Hill and , irresistibly , Kate Winslet was rumoured to be in talks to play Dusty Springfield . The BBC , knowing it was on to a good thing , moved bigger biopic projects over to BBC Two . Here , Julie Walters starred as Mary Whitehouse and Victoria Wood wrote a touching , Bafta-winning play Eric and Ernie , based on her comedy heroes . There were efforts , too , to extend the series ' range beyond the world of light entertainment . Winnie Mandela , Alexander Fleming and Baroness Thatcher all came under scrutiny . There was also a concerted effort to play with the format , taking it beyond the realm of the straight biopic . In 2008 , the oddly structured Consuming Passion told the story of publishing house Mills and Boon across three eras . More successful were The Road to Coronation Street ( 2010 ) , an ensemble piece which charted the origins of the ITV soap and the meticulously crafted Holy Flying Circus ( @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ when Monty Python 's John Cleese and Michael Palin appeared on a talk show to defend Life of Brian in front of an unyielding Bishop of Southwark . Here , the drama took on Pythonesque qualities with Terry Gilliam-style animation sequences and absurd captioning that commented on the action . Rufus Jones who played Terry Jones was , as befitted the original Python shows , required to drag up as John Cleese 's wife . Despite some very strong reviews and multiple Bafta nominations , it seems that Burton and Taylor is to be the last BBC Four biopic . When I asked to interview Janice Hadlow , the current controller of BBC Two who oversaw BBC Four at the height of its drama output , my request was politely declined . Perhaps Hadlow feared difficult questions about the future of a channel that is , admittedly , no longer under her jurisdiction . Following severe budget cuts , original drama will disappear altogether from BBC Four , and yet , somehow , you wonder whether something so clearly shot on a shoestring as The Road to Coronation Street @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ dwindling finances . BBC Four budget biopics could be inspiring , and clearly they have inspired other broadcasters . ITV have just finished filming a life of Tommy Cooper starring David Threlfall and , in the autumn , Sky Atlantic will show Fleming , based on the life of the James Bond creator . It seems , then , that the demand is there , but with BBC Four withdrawing from the dramatic arena , it has lost ownership of a genre that it crafted so brilliantly , and clearly made its own . There are so many possible subjects still out there -- my personal wish list would have included Opportunity Knocks star Lena Zavaroni , Flanders and Swann , Ronald Searle , Frank Bough . Sadly , none of these will now be made . The BBC Four biopic has been cut off in its prime . It ends , though , with two great British stars playing two luminous British screen legends .

URL: https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/news/reporting-interest-income-i-t-dept-message-to-revise-itr-baffles-tax-payers/article34208713.ece
COUNTRY: in
CONTINENT: Asia

TITLE: Reporting interest income: I-T Dept message to `revise ...

CONTENT: ? Project checks discrepancies in reporting of interest income earned of over ? 5,000 Hundreds of bank deposit holders across the country were non-plussed on Wednesday by an Income Tax Department message to check for discrepancies in reporting their interest income . Identified as high-value information under a new functionality called ' Special Pilot Project ( SPP ) -Interest ' , the account holders were told to clear " tax dues " by March 31 . The I-T department had launched the pilot project to check for discrepancies in reporting interest income by taxpayers . Under the project , the department had asked banks to provide details of customers who had earned interest income of over ? 5,000 in Assessment Year 2020-21 . On matching the data sourced from the banks with that of the income-tax returns filed , the department detected under-reporting and sent out emails and SMSes to the assessees to rectify the mistake . " The Income Tax Department has identified high-value information which does not appear to be in line with the Income Tax Return filed for Assessment Year 2020-21 ( relating to FY 2019-20 ) . Please revise @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ CP ) . Access CP by logging into e-filing portal and clicking on ' Compliance Portal ' link under ' My Account ' or ' Compliance ' tab - ITD , " reads the SMS sent to assessees over the past few days . This left a number of bank account holders , especially senior citizens , anxious . " I received the message on the weekend and Monday was a holiday . How does the Tax Department expect senior citizens and pensioners to check discrepancies , rectify them and pay the dues by March 31 . In my case , I checked with the bank and there is a case of double counting , " said an octogenarian residing in Mumbai . Double counting " Banks had shared the details of cumulative interest that exceeds ? 5,000 per person in FY 2019-20 to the Income Tax office as per its specified format . It is observed that some amount reported in high-value transaction is duplication of entries , and customers are approaching the banks to rectify the same . The matter has been taken up with @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ to rectify it at the earliest , " said a banking source Mihir Ashok Tanna , Associate Director ( Direct tax ) , SK Patodia and Associates , an audit firm , said the Central Board of Direct Taxation is flooded with requests to extend the deadline for filing the revised return for the past fiscal as the website crashed on Wednesday . In most cases , banks have provided wrong data on interest earned by customers and there were also mistakes in entry of customers ' PAN details and interest income , adding to the confusion , he added . Another tax expert said that in some cases , the Tax Department 's communication is legitimate . TITLE: Inside the mind of John Dobson

CONTENT: South African rugby news , fixtures , results , video , interviews , and more . Covering the Springboks , Bulls , Stormers/Western Province , Sharks , Cheetahs , Kings and Lions , as well as schools and club rugby . The incoming Stormers coach shares a few of his favourite pastimes and adventures away from the game . FAVOURITE ALBUMS DISINTEGRATION -- THE CURE ' Plainsong , Closedown and Pictures of You are all anthems . This is my favourite album of all time by a mile . ' STOP MAKING SENSE -- TALKING HEADS ' David Byrne in his big suit was a record I scratched and rebought three times . I still have the vinyl . ' YOU 'VE COME A LONG WAY , BABY -- FATBOY SLIM ' His real name is Norman Cook and he was unbelievably cool in the London Olympics opening ceremony . ' FAVOURITE BANDS THE CURE ' If it 's a dress-up party I will go as Robert Smith , with a wig and make-up ! ' DEPECHE MODE ' Their techno music helped change music and it 's remarkable that @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ sharp end . ' SPRINGBOK NUDE GIRLS ' They evoke great memories of fun along the coast in the ' 90s . ' FAVOURITE BOOKS PORTRAIT OF THE ARTIST AS A YOUNG DOG -- DYLAN THOMAS ' I love " Under Milkwood " and the moving poem " And Death shall have no dominion " . He wrote about fighting against ageing and death , yet died at 39 . He drank a lot of beer . ' BENNIE GRIESEL SERIES -- DEON MEYER ' Deon stands up internationally as a crime writer and has helped the genre grow so big in SA . He is a national treasure and a good ou . ' YEAR OF THE GHERKIN , YEAR OF THE TURNIP , YEAR OF THE BEETROOT -- JOHN DOBSON ' My own books ! The first two are sold out , the third is n't out yet . ' PEOPLE I 'D LIKE TO MEET STEPHEN FRY ' A brain like that is awe-inspiring . ' ROBERT MUGABE ' He has some explaining to do . ' CYRIL @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ ? ' FAVOURITE CITIES VISITED VERONA , ITALY ' I played rugby there and visit as often as I can . It 's in Romeo and Juliet : " There is no world without Verona walls " . ' CHICAGO , USA ' I did the marathon there . I did n't enjoy the running , but it 's a vibrant city with a great vibe and fantastic watersports . ' LAMU ISLAND , KENYA ' I had an unbelievable holiday there , with great windsurfing , sailing and culture . I just named a new dog after it . ' FAVOURITE SPORTING MOMENTS 2005 ASHES SERIES ' I 'm not English nor Australian , but that was a wonderful Test series . ' 2008 VARSITY CUP FINAL ' We did n't win UCT lost 16-10 to Maties , but it was the first year of the competition and fantastic to be a part of . ' 1974 RUMBLE IN THE JUNGLE ' The fact that this fight between Muhammad Ali and George Foreman took place in Zaire in the ' 70s @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ world and what a fight it was ! ' FAVOURITE QUOTES DYLAN THOMAS ' Do not go gentle into that good night , Old age should burn and rave at close of day ; Rage , rage against the dying of the light . ' SAMUEL JOHNSON ' Chains of habit are too light to be felt until they are too heavy to be broken . ' HENRY V ( SHAKESPEARE ) ' We few , we happy few , we band of brothers -- for whoever sheds his blood with me today shall be my brother . ' BEST JOBS COACHING WESTERN PROVINCE ' I 've always been a WP fan , and to be the team 's coach is an honour , privilege and responsibility . ' STARTING 365 DIGITAL PUBLISHING ' Speed matters in publishing , and the web makes it easier to get content out to your audience . ' HOSTING KURT COBAIN ' This when Nirvana played in Lisbon . It was eye opening , to say the least . '

TITLE: Police want to speak to this man following indecent exposure on footpath

CONTENT: Get the latest MEN breaking news on WhatsApp Police have released an e-fit image after a man exposed himself on a public footpath in Wilmslow . Officers are investigating an indecent exposure that took place on a path off Dean Row Road , adjacent to the Colshaw Farm estate . Cheshire Police has today ( November 4 ) released an e-fit showing a man they would like to speak to in connection with the incident , which took place at around 5.45pm on Tuesday , October 8 . The suspect is described as white , in his mid-30s and with an oval face which was pock-marked , especially round the chin . At the time of the incident , he was wearing mid-blue denim jeans which were unclean and a navy-blue raincoat , which was mid-bottom-length . The jacket had a hood with a capped peak at the front and appeared grubby . Detective Constable Gary Thomas , of Cheshire Police , said : " I 'd urge anyone who recognises the man , or the man himself , to contact us so that we @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ make enquiries in the area and would urge anyone with information to get in touch . " Anyone with information should contact Cheshire Police on 101 or online , quoting IML 1936066 . Alternatively , information can be given anonymously by calling Crimestoppers on 0800 555 111 . TITLE: Profile: The Salvation Army director details love of volunteering

CONTENT: Profile : The Salvation Army director details love of volunteering Stephen Thomas has worked as the Denton County Social Services Director for The Salvation Army for 15 months . Among his many responsibilities is to help lead the newly formed Denton County emergency disaster services team that deploys a food canteen supporting first responder and victims of any natural disaster event as well as participating in other community events . In addition , the family man has helped raise three children , who are also in public service roles . Tell us about your family . My wife , Julia and I have been married for 26 years . She works at Washington Federal Bank as their retail banking division manager for Texas . We have three children . Our oldest son , Kevin proudly serves the community of Flower Mound as a firefighter/paramedic for the past nine years . Our daughter , Sara , is a registered nurse at Medical City in Denton and our youngest son , Nathan just completed the fire academy with the plan to follow his brother as a firefighter/paramedic . How are you involved with the community ? @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ we have lived in . We have volunteered at church youth ministries , food banks , Habitat for Humanity and homeless outreach programs . I was a volunteer with the Red Kettle campaign at the Salvation Army long before I was hired . Serving brings joy . What are some of your hobbies ? Biking , sailing and travel . I have been all continents except Antarctica . What are your responsibilities as Denton County Social Services Director ? My role is to be the Denton County representative for Salvation Army including the day-to-day operations of the Lewisville Service Center , Denton Homeless Shelter . I participate in public relations activities in the Denton and Lewisville communities to promote The Salvation Army needs and create awareness of all the services how we are able to assist our community . Some of these areas include providing meals , providing family pantry food assistance , clothing assistance , employment assistance and assistance navigating social service referrals . The Salvation Army collaborates with many local groups , government offices , community churches , city leaders , nonprofit partners and @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ many in our community including those currently homeless , displaced veterans and hardworking family who need help . Why did you first get involved with the Salvation Army ? I have always been a supporter of the good work that the Salvation Army does in their communities including their good stewardship of every dollar donated . I have been a volunteer with their Red Kettle campaign each Christmas season for quite awhile . I find great joy in the fact that I now work for an organization that I have long admired . Have you always had a philanthropic spirit ? Yes , it is part of my family DNA . While growing up in California my parents would promote helping others working often with various organizations and individuals . What is one life lesson you have learned over the years ? From the Bible : Others ... Mathew 25:40 Truly , I say to you , as you did it to one of the least of these my brothers , you did it to me . If you could sit in with @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ choose ? Mercy Me If you could live in any sitcom , which one would it be ? Parks and Recreation because I love communities . What 's the first thing you do when you wake up Saturday morning ? TITLE: Where Plymouth's homeless have gone - and what will happen ...

CONTENT: When you subscribe we will use the information you provide to send you these newsletters . Sometimes they 'll include recommendations for other related newsletters or services we offer . OurPrivacy Noticeexplains more about how we use your data , and your rights . You can unsubscribe at any time . Weeks ago , several of the city 's most vital services took part in a great effort to help 26 rough sleepers off our streets and into shelter . This was accomplished in just six days . A staggering result . For the most part this was achieved by an alliance of Plymouth 's service providers , read more here . The contract for this collaboration was formed in April 2019 . Plymouth City Council is a partner and the other organisations are Harbour , Hamoaze House , The Zone , Bournemouth Churches Housing Association ( BCHA ) , Plymouth Access to Housing ( PATH ) , Shekinah , Livewell Southwest . Their leadership team had a meeting on May 5 to discuss the next stage of planning , the gathering of information and the impact of the past six to eight @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ she pulls together information from the alliance members . The effort to get people into accommodation was a success , but what will be available for homeless people when lockdown ends ? Looking forward , Christine said : " We 're in discussions , and the intention is that people will not be put back on the streets . But we do not have a clear plan . " The intention is that we find appropriate accommodation and we continue to house and support people to maintain tenancies . " As you can imagine , those individuals who were sleeping rough maybe have n't made applications for benefits they could 've had . What is These Four Walls ? PlymouthLive , CornwallLive and DevonLive launch new lockdown social isolation campaign The lockdown has changed all of our lives - in ways we could never have imagined . It happened in a matter of days . One minute we were allowed to leave our homes at will , the next we were not . Seeing friends and family has always been our right @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ we love in the hope it will save lives . And for many of us , being confined within the four walls of our homes has had dire consequences - financially , mentally or physically . This is why we are launching These Four Walls , a campaign which aims to inform , educate and share important , harrowing and inspiring stories from the coronavirus lockdown in Devon and Cornwall . Each week we will be focusing on a different issue and raising money for a different charity , starting with the Samaritans to coincide with their own campaign Our Frontline . Our four walls may be very real , but it 's through sharing our experiences , stories and struggles that we all remain connected . " Maybe they have n't engaged with what it is they want to do with their lives moving forward . " Having chatted to one gentleman who I met previously at the drop-in centre in Shekinah , he was just delighted to have his own room , his own bed and his own duvet . Even the @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ normal ' and that 's what we want to be normal for everybody in the city . " What we 'll be doing is looking to see how we allocate budgets to maintain the current position and enhance it by working in partnership with others . " She described the accommodation essentially as ' student housing ' and they are multi-occupancy with access to bathroom facilities . Teams working for the services have moved from their usual jobs and some have taken up roles in these houses . Efforts have been made to ensure that those who are often the most vulnerable have homes , food and are engaging . Sign up to our newsletters for all the latest near you For more of the latest news , sign up to receive one of our daily or weekly newsletters . Our teams at Cornwall Live , Devon Live and Plymouth Live are committed to bringing you the latest news , guiding you through this global pandemic and providing the stories that matter . Our daily news email goes out every afternoon and offers @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ also sign up for the weekend report , to receive the top stories on a Saturday morning . Or if you wanted something more specific , sign up to read our Truro , Exeter and Torbay newsletters . Choose which newsletters apply to you from across our Devon and Cornwall titles here - so that you can stay informed , stay home and stay safe . Christine continued : " What the pandemic has enabled us to do is break our system further to put it back together in a way which is better for those people . " At the beginning of March we were able to get everybody , without shelter in the city , offered a place to stay . " In total we are at around 75 additional beds in the city . Staff across the alliance have been incredible . " PATH is a member of the alliance , Mike Taylor is the director . He acknowledged the challenge Plymouth faces to house people , and anticipates a strain could be felt until the end of the year . @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ housed now , many of those were in accommodation . They were in communal spaces . " With COVID-19 we 've been told , and rightly so , that everyone should have their own room . It 's been a huge piece of work , but done very quickly . Read More Related Articles Read More Related Articles You can be considered homeless if you 're without a legal right to live where you are staying . You do n't have to be sleeping rough , you could be on a friend 's sofa . People in this situation also need somewhere permanent to stay . The stress of lockdown could result in relationships breaking down and informal agreements such as a friend staying over could come to an end . These people , in addition to rough sleepers , to people fleeing violent relationships , awaiting eviction , will all need somewhere to stay . Mike continued : " There 's so much need for people to be accommodated . It 's really difficult to get people their own tenancies @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ " It 's legally and practically a complicated thing to do , and time consuming . People need rent in advance , and deposits . " We still try to do that , and are managing to house some people that way . But there 's a constant stream of people presenting as homeless to the city . " Many of those wo n't have slept rough and we really want to make sure they do n't . " As PATH alone , we 've got another 44 rooms we 've taken on in the last few weeks to accommodate as part of what we 're doing collectively . " We 're trying to make sure that most of those houses and rooms are not short term because we ca n't be in a position where after lockdown we 've suddenly got not accommodation left for people . " There will be ongoing work , and we 'll be working frantically to cope with the people who are in temporary accommodation who we want to move to more settled accommodation . " The government @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ ' It will be intense for the rest of this calendar year for sure ' " When that comes to an end there will be a lot of people due to be evicted , for whatever reason , presenting as homeless to local councils and charities . " It would be naive to think that it 's going to get easier . It 's going to be very complicated . We do n't want to lose the progress we 've made . " There will be more people coming forward . This has been an intense period and it will be differently intense for the rest of this calendar year for sure . " Despite difficulties ahead , Mike 's outlook remains optimistic , he said : " Just last week we took on a 12 bedroom house and I think we 've leased it for three years . " We 've got to be able to say that actually you do n't all have to leave . " We need to make sure as much as possible that when lockdown ends , @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ services . " Those houses leased for three months could potentially go back , but we 'll be moving people into their own accommodation where we can .

TITLE: Stassi Schroeder Gives Birth to Baby No. 2 With Husband Beau Clark

CONTENT: The ' Vanderpump Rules ' alum and her husband welcomed a baby boy . Schroeder shared the exciting news Saturday on Instagram while also revealing the couple has named the baby boy Messer Rhys Clark . Schroeder also revealed the boy was born four minutes after midnight on Sept. 7 . Messer weighed a healthy 7 pounds , 14 ounces . " We love him so much already , send prayers Hartford does n't terrorize him , " she added in her caption . " I knew the whole entire time that we were having a boy , " Schroeder exclaimed with enthusiasm during the podcast . " I kept saying it . I felt it . It was a vibe . " The reality star turned podcast host went on to elaborate about her intuition , attributing it to being " so in tune with her body . " This connection led her to become " so convinced " that she was expecting a son . Schroeder initially announced her second pregnancy with a heartfelt Instagram post , expressing her love for her unborn child . Unfortunately , the post has since been @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ The couple first tied the knot in an intimate ceremony in October 2020 , but their larger wedding celebration was postponed due to the COVID-19 pandemic . At the time , Schroeder was already pregnant with Hartford . They eventually had their dream wedding in Italy in May 2022 . Stassi Schroeder/InstagramBeau Clark/Instagram Schroeder 's first pregnancy announcement in 2020 came amid controversy . She , along with co-star Kristin Doute , were fired from Vanderpump Rules due to their involvement in racist actions against their former co-star , Faith Stowers . Schroeder publicly apologized for her past actions and took steps to educate herself about race and diversity , even hiring a diversity coach . Reflecting on her journey , she explained , " I have been out of this situation and this environment for three years now ... Drama is not my life anymore . I do n't know how to digest it . I do n't know how to talk about it . "

TITLE: The Orlando Magic spat in the face of Black players by supporting Ron DeSantis

CONTENT: The Orlando Magic spat in the face of Black players by supporting Ron DeSantis The NBA team 's backing of a US presidential candidate who has crusaded to delegitimize the horrors of slavery is hypocritical and disgusting Mon 7 Aug 2023 05.00 EDTLast modified on Mon 7 Aug 2023 09.55 EDT The Orlando Magic 's decision to make a $50,000 contribution in support of the presidential bid of Florida 's Republican governor , Ron DeSantis , sends a clear message to their Black players , employees and fans . And that message is a U-turn from the one in the summer of 2020 when the United States was in an uproar over the police killings of George Floyd , Breonna Taylor , the shooting of Jacob Blake as well as countless other examples of police brutality . " We stand with our neighbors and the nation in condemning the horrific and senseless death of George Floyd . There have been far too many victims of racial bias and abuses of power , particularly in the Black community . As an organization , we remain deeply committed to providing an environment where all feel welcomed @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ dignity and respect . " But the team 's recent decision to throw their support ( and money ) behind the campaign of DeSantis is the antithesis is everything the Magic claimed to support less than three years ago . Let me be clear . I am in no way , shape or form suggesting that the Magic and their governors , the DeVos family , do n't have the right to support whichever presidential candidate they choose , just as NBA players have the right to support whichever candidate they choose . My book , We Matter : Athletes And Activism , encouraged and pushed for athletes to continue the rich tradition of sportspeople using their voices and their platforms to take a stand on issues that affect our communities . So it would be hypocritical for me to now say that the Magic do n't have that same right . Of course they do . However , I also have the right to point out the hypocrisy of claiming to be " deeply committed " to equality , and dedicated to rooting out " racial @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ " , then offering financial support to a person like DeSantis , the Republican governor who has dedicated himself to the very ideals the Magic claimed to oppose . Ironically , after the summer of 2020 , the Orlando Magic were also a part of the group of NBA governors which formed the Social Justice Coalition to display their alleged commitment to tackling many of the issues addressed at protests across the US . The coalition 's concerns involved issues such as police brutality , police accountability , voting rights , criminal justice reform , the education system and dismantling racism , bias and discrimination -- areas that disproportionately affect Black people in particular . And in Florida , one of the main people in direct opposition to Black people as a whole is none other than DeSantis , whose policies have made a full embrace of racism , bias and bigotry . DeSantis backed a slew of new laws passed by the state legislature that are focused on removing what he termed as " woke indoctrination " in an effort to " empower " white parents @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ race theory , an intellectual movement that examines the way policies and laws perpetuate systemic racism . He also forbid teachers from teaching any material that makes white students " feel guilt " because of actions committed by white people of the past . The NAACP issued a travel warning to Black people because of DeSantis 's " openly hostility " toward Black people in the state of Florida . DeSantis 's administration also approved a new curriculum requiring schools to teach that slaves developed skills that " could be applied for their personal benefit . " ( I ca n't even wrap my mind around how any decent human being could even suggest that slavery was a benefit to the people actually enslaved . The mere thought is beyond the pale , but to enact a curriculum to teach that to children is evil . ) I remember as a teenager asking my grandmother about a book she was reading called Roots . She told me through tears stories that were passed down to her by her grandmother of the horrors of slavery . It was @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ was more of an actual horror movie . Torture , rape , public floggings , castrations , lynchings , beatings , mutilation , families torn apart , men and women worked in the fields from " ca n't see in the morning until ca n't see at night " . I remember my grandmother saying " they did n't view us as human beings . " There are no silver linings from slavery . It was hell on earth . I could n't imagine playing for an organization that would support the disrespectful whitewashing and watering-down of my ancestors ' tragic history in this country . It would be like asking a Jewish person to work for a Holocaust denier . Here is the bottom line : DeSantis is running his campaign on racism . The goal of his slogan " Florida is where woke goes to die " is crystal clear . He is Trump 2.0 . His dog whistles are n't even discreet ; they are obvious hate-filled declarations . But in addition to his words , his actual policies are patterned after white supremacy @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ the Jim Crow south not only in his language , which would be dangerous enough , but in his actual laws . So for the Magic to make a contribution to him , and in support of his whistling-Dixie , Confederate , Jim Crow aspirations , they are choosing the antithesis of freedom , justice and equality . The direct opposition to " creating an environment where all feel welcomed , valued , appreciated and are all treated with dignity and respect " . It was good to see the NBA players ' union voice their disapproval in a statement on Wednesday . " A political contribution from the Orlando Magic is alarming given recent comments and policies of its beneficiary , " read the statement . " NBA governors , players and personnel have the right to express their personal political views , including through donations and statements . " However , if contributions are made on behalf of an entire team , using money earned through the labor of its employees , it is incumbent upon the team governors to consider the diverse values @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ donation does not represent player support for the recipient , " the union said . That pretty much sums it up . By consciously aligning themselves with white supremacy , the Magic have spit in the face of every Black player on their roster , every Black coach on their bench , every Black employee of their organization and every Black fan of their team .

TITLE: Eisa shines as Al Ain hold Duhail

CONTENT: Eisa shines as Al Ain hold Duhail 04/08/2019 Tell your friends UAE giants Al Ain bounced back from a two-goal deficit to clinch a thrilling 2-2 away draw against Qatar 's Al Duhail in the AFC Champions League on Monday.Al Ain players mob goalkeeper Khalid Eisa after his heroics allowed them to snatch a draw in Qatar Karim Boudiaf had put the hosts ahead in the ninth minute of the Group C match at the Abdullah bin Khalifa Stadium in Doha as Al Duhail looked to bounce back from the disappointment of finishing second to Al Sadd in the Qatar Stars League . But after the prolific Youssef El Arabi helped Rui Faria 's side consolidate with a 32nd-minute strike , Al Ain hit their groove and began clawing back into the game . They reaped an immediate reward for their efforts when Jamal Maroof pounced on a headed pass from Tongo Doumbia after receiving a long ball from goalkeeper Khalid Eisa and calmly found the mark in the 35th minute . Al Duhail came close to restoring their two-goal advantage but goalkeeper Eisa produced a stunning save from six yards @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Cup which Qatar won earlier this year . Eisa in fact was the star of the match on Monday , pulling off an array of brilliant saves to frustrate Al Duhail and help his side stay alive in the contest . Al Ain were the more industrious side in the second half and created several opportunities , although they failed to take advantage of a penalty in the 53rd minute as Duhail goalkeeper Amine Lecomte guessed correctly and thwarted Caio 's shot after Maroof was brought down by Mohammed Musa . Lecomte , however , went from hero to villain in a matter of minutes as he failed to collect Maroof 's cross cleanly and Marcus Berg equalised before the hour . Eisa later produced another fine save from El Arabi in the last minute to thwart the hosts . Meanwhile , Iran 's Zobahan shrugged off an early goal deficit to outclass the UAE 's Al Wasl 3-1 at the Zabeel Stadium in Dubai . Brazilian Caio had put Al Wasl ahead in the 14th minute but the hosts fell apart after that @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ ( 35 ) and Hamid Bouhamdan ( 86 ) all found the target to give Zobahan a comfortable win in Group A.

TITLE: Having trouble sleeping? Orgasms can serve as natural sleep therapy

CONTENT: Having trouble sleeping ? Orgasms can serve as natural sleep therapy We 've all been there at some point : tossing and turning throughout the late night in hopes of finding a comfortable position to fall asleep , all the while getting more anxious and frustrated as the minutes on the clock slowly inch toward the time where your alarm will go off . While there are many reasons for a lack of quality sleep , there are also several natural and easy ways to relax your mind and body for a restful slumber . One such effective course of action ? Giving yourself -- or having a partner give you -- an orgasm . " Generally , an orgasm helps one to relax and relieve stress , while the refractory period post-orgasm allows for the feeling of release and a lull into a more restful state , " explains Andrea Tan , a Singapore-based certified sexual wellness and relationship coach . " After we orgasm , our brain releases serotonin , which encourages a feeling of relaxation that helps us to switch off before bed . Serotonin is a cortisol killer , " she explains . " Cortisol is a stress hormone that typically rises throughout the day , especially if we have been up against stressful situations . " Reducing your cortisol level before bed is important to help prevent a vicious cycle of anxiety and insomnia , says Gemma . " If we repeatedly put ourselves to bed whilst feeling stressed and occupied by negative thoughts , it conditions our brains to believe that the bedroom is a stressful place and can lead to sleep issues such as chronic insomnia , " she warns . The sleep consultant , who firmly believes in a holistic and science-based approach to help adults and children sleep better , is collaborating with global pleasure brand We-Vibe to promote a positive relationship between sleep and sex .
